description: Dialga serves as the secondary anti-dragon behind Xerneas, covering Groudon/dragon swaps so Kyogre can freely two-shield and dominate the rest of the meta.

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: I went 13-2 in Master League with this team | Pokémon GO PvP

description: A bulky defensive backbone — in a league where everything hits extremely hard, Lugia and Dialga are highlighted as two of the very few Pokémon that can take a hit from a Mega, so they pair together as a safe back line behind a Mega attacker.

sources.author: YasserPoGO
sources.videoTitle: TOP TEAMS in the Mega Master League | Pokemon GO PvP

description: Double steel backline core forming the backbone of the classic Dragonite double steel archetype. Both Steel types complement Dragonite's Dragon typing — Empoleon provides Water/Steel coverage while Excadrill provides Ground/Steel coverage.

sources.author: Reis2TheOccasion
sources.videoTitle: INSANELY HIGH ATTACK TEAM WITH SHADOW EXCADRILL!! | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Eternatus provides the fastest dragon damage to pressure Rayquaza/Zekrom while Kyogre handles the fairy/steel threats and mops up with Origin Pulse, giving the team strong matchups into the dominant Master League meta.

sources.author: HomeSliceHenry
sources.videoTitle: *LEVEL 50* Eternatus is a MENACE! | Pokémon GO Battle League

description: Pseudo-dragon backline core — Gyarados baits out opposing dragons who don't expect Twister, softening them up or forcing switches for Garchomp to clean up. Both threaten dragons while covering different typings (Water/Flying + Dragon/Ground).

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: No Legendaries? No Problem! Try this BUDGET TEAM in the Master League!!

description: The two reliable answers held in the back behind Baxcalibur — Primal Kyogre is nearly impossible to safely switch into (bolt-beam style coverage), and Ho-Oh with Brave Bird cleans up the mega water types and hard counters that trouble Kyogre.

sources.author: JonkusPKMN
sources.videoTitle: I HUNTED down PRIMAL KYOGRE with *NEW* GLAIVE RUSH Baxcalibur in the Mega Master League!

description: Explicitly called a 'very good core' by the creator. Marshadow's Fighting/Ghost typing complements Kyogre's Water typing, helping cover each other's weaknesses in the Master League meta.

sources.author: XehrFelroseGO
sources.videoTitle: HUGE IMPROVEMENT!! BUFFED KYOGRE is BETTER THAN EVER in the Master League!!

description: Xerneas is specifically chosen over Zacian because it beats the key dragons (Palkia, Reshiram, Zekrom) that threaten Kyogre. Creator explicitly explains: 'Zacian doesn't really beat the good dragons... Xerneas has winning matchups into all of them.'

description: Landorus Therian's guaranteed attack debuff from Sandsear Storm softens opponents so Kyurem Black can tank hits it normally couldn't survive (e.g. Xerneas Moon Blast), while Kyurem Black's Shadow Claw + Fusion Bolt coverage handles fairy types that threaten Landorus.

description: Double Dragon core — two powerful dragons that complement Zacian, providing heavy neutral damage and coverage. Creator explicitly calls out 'Zacian plus double dragon, hard to go wrong with that' as the team's strategic foundation.

sources.videoTitle: Zekrom Is The BEST Pokémon In Master League [Pokémon GO PvP]

description: Landorus acts as safe swap to lure out water types (Palkia, Kyogre), allowing Metagross to sweep in the back with Meteor Mash and Earthquake once those threats are removed. ABB-style synergy where both share ground-type coverage roles.

description: Xerneas lead with Lunala safe switch — Lunala punishes opponents bringing dragon-type safe switches into Xerneas, and the switch dynamic desyncs timers to give Xerneas favorable realignments.
